board.title: Classical Natural Language Processing
board.author: Julius Boateng
board.description: Natural language processing exists at the intersection of linguistics and computer science. The early foundations took shape in the 1940s and accelerated in the 1990s as research shifted from rule-based systems toward statistical language modeling.

clues.question: Subfield of NLP focused on interpreting meaning beyond surface-level syntax
Smallest unit of sound that changes meaning in spoken language; foundational to speech recognition
Changed the framework for understanding language as a structured rule-based system in his 1957 landmark paper "Syntactic Structures"
Tree representing the syntactic structure of a string according to a context-free grammar
Simulated conversational program developed at MIT in 1964 that reflected users' statements back as questions; origin of the ___ effect
Process of reducing inflected words to their base dictionary form, or lemma; e.g. "studies" → "study"
Created a test for whether computers could convincingly imitate human conversation in his 1950 paper "Computing Machinery and Intelligence"
Established the idea that language could be modeled statistically in his 1948 paper "A Mathematical Theory of Communication"; 1972 Turing Award winner
Process of splitting raw text into smaller units called tokens before language analysis
Process of reducing words to their root form by removing prefixes and suffixes; e.g. "studies" → "studi"
Collection of authentic text used to analyze patterns in language; the 1961 Brown Corpus was an early influential digital text collection
Probabilistic model where future states depend on the current state; foundational to statistical language modeling
Contiguous sequence of N words or characters used in statistical language modeling
